data.title: Bessant: The new Federal Reserve Chair may be announced in about a week.
data.description: According to Yahoo Finance, U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant said that Trump's nominee for Federal Reserve Chair could be decided in about a week.

data.title: Significant SOL Transfer from Bitgo Custody to Anonymous Address
data.description: A substantial transfer of SOL tokens has been reported. According to ChainCatcher, at 03:30, 40,000 SOL were moved from Bitgo Custody to an anonymous address starting with Dz6KMc. The transaction highlights ongoing activity in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Japanese yen slides to historic lows as U.S. rejects currency intervention
data.description:
The Japanese yen dropped further in value against the dollar following U. S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s refusal to intervene in saving the faltering currency. The USD/JPY pair has fallen to historic lows recently due to policy decisions by the Japanese government and central bank. This news signifies a growing divergence between U. S. and Japanese monetary […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/japanese-yen-slides-to-historic-lows-as-u-s-rejects-currency-intervention/

data.title: Federal Reserve Adjusts Employment Risk Language Amid Labor Market Stability
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has announced the removal of language concerning downside employment risks from the Fed's latest policy statement. According to NS3.AI, this change reflects signs of stability in the labor market. Despite this positive development, Powell cautioned investors and policymakers against over-interpreting the current employment data. His remarks indicate a cautious optimism regarding job market conditions amid ongoing economic uncertainties.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Undecided on Future Board Role
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has not yet decided whether he will continue to serve on the Board of Governors after his term as chair concludes. According to NS3.AI, Powell's current term as a board member is set to last until 2028. He declined to comment on a subpoena and highlighted that the responsibility for managing the value of the U.S. dollar lies with the Treasury Department, not the Federal Reserve.

data.title: BREAKING: Fed Chair Jerome Powell Speaks LIVE After Interest Rate Decision – Details Here
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is making statements live on air after interest rates were left unchanged as expected. Continue Reading: BREAKING: Fed Chair Jerome Powell Speaks LIVE After Interest Rate Decision – Details Here

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: If tariff inflation peaks and then falls, it would indicate that we can ease policy.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: If we see this (tariff inflation peaking and then falling), it will indicate that we can ease policy. (Golden Ten)

data.title: Institutions: Latest FOMC statement may signal that the Fed will refocus on inflation.
data.description: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged as expected and stated that its March decision would depend on upcoming data. Sid Vaidya, a wealth management analyst at TD Securities, said the statement acknowledged strong GDP growth and a stable unemployment rate, raising questions about how much the Fed will prioritize persistently high inflation. Recent rate cuts have supported employment. Therefore, Vaidya suspects the latest statement may signal a renewed focus on inflation by the Fed. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Positioned for Flexible Rate Adjustments, Says Powell
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell has stated that the central bank is in a favorable position to determine the extent and timing of additional interest rate adjustments. According to ChainCatcher, Powell emphasized that the policy is not following a predetermined path, allowing for flexibility in decision-making.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell Attributes Inflation to Tariff-Driven Goods
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has indicated that the current high inflation levels are largely driven by tariff-affected goods. According to ChainCatcher, Powell's remarks highlight the significant impact of tariffs on inflationary pressures.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Comments on Government Shutdown Impact
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has indicated that the recent government shutdown is likely to have hindered economic growth in the fourth quarter. According to ChainCatcher, Powell noted that while the shutdown may have slowed growth, its effects are expected to be reversed in the future.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Discusses U.S. Economic Stability
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell addressed the public on January 29, stating that the U.S. economy remains fundamentally strong. According to BlockBeats, Powell noted that the economy expanded at a steady pace last year. He also mentioned signs of stabilization in the unemployment rate.

Powell emphasized that the current policy stance is appropriate. He acknowledged that the recent government shutdown likely impacted economic growth in the fourth quarter, but he expects this effect to be reversed.

data.title: UK Watchdog Bans Coinbase Ads for Linking Crypto to Cost-of-Living Crisis
data.description:
The UK’s advertising regulator has banned a high-profile marketing campaign by Coinbase, ruling that the ads irresponsibly implied cryptocurrency could help address the country’s cost-of-living pressures. The Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) concluded that the campaign breached social responsibility rules by trivializing financial risk at a time of widespread economic strain.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/uk-watchdog-bans-coinbase-ads-for-linking-crypto-to-cost-of-living-crisis/

data.description:

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: The U.S. economy is on solid foundations, and current policies are helping to make progress toward achieving our two main goals.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell stated that the U.S. economy has a solid foundation, having expanded at a steady pace last year, and will enter 2026 on a solid basis. The unemployment rate has shown some signs of stabilizing. Current policies are helping to make progress towards achieving the two main goals. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: Policy rates are within a reasonable range of estimates of the neutral rate.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: Policy rates are within a reasonable range of estimates of the neutral rate. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: The Fed is unlikely to cut interest rates before Powell leaves office; Waller is trying to get Trump's attention.
data.description: Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group, said the Fed didn't make any waves, and the market widely expected them to press the pause button. We probably won't see any rate cuts until Powell leaves the Fed in May. The potential good news is that they mentioned some positive factors regarding the labor market, but inflation is clearly still a concern. We know Milan entered the Fed to stir things up, but Waller's choice is somewhat intriguing. His name is still on the list of potential next chair candidates, so I'm sure he's also trying to get Trump's attention to show he remains firmly in the dovish camp. The Fed's independence is indeed a real issue, and the next chair will face enormous pressure to cut rates. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairmanship Speculations Amid Waller's Opposition Vote
data.description:
Currently, Christopher Waller's dissenting vote is being linked to his potential candidacy for the Federal Reserve Chair position. According to Odaily, Pepperstone analyst Michael Brown suggests that this might keep Waller's slim chances of succeeding Chairman Jerome Powell alive for now.

Polymarket data indicates that Kevin Warsh has a 28% probability of being elected as the Federal Reserve Chair, Rick Rieder's chances have risen to 39%, Kevin Hassett's probability stands at 6%, and Christopher Waller's chances are at 12%.
